visuals.title: Deadlock Characterization & Resource-Allocation Graph
visuals.description: Animated diagram showing the four necessary conditions for deadlock (Mutual Exclusion, Hold and Wait, No Preemption, Circular Wait) and a Resource-Allocation Graph demonstrating a deadlock cycle with single-instance resources.

items.label: Mutual Exclusion
items.description: At least one resource must be non-sharable; only one process can use it at a time.

items.label: Hold and Wait
items.description: A process holds at least one resource while waiting for additional resources held by other processes.

items.label: No Preemption
items.description: Resources cannot be forcibly taken away; a process must voluntarily release its resources.

items.label: Circular Wait
items.description: A chain of processes exists such that each waits for a resource held by the next, forming a closed loop.
